extensions.name: http_metadata
extensions.description: Metadata extracting from an HTTP packet. For example: detects HTTP Pipeline, decodes the Authorization-Credentials header value, and so on.

extensions.name: hostname
extensions.description: Hostname extracting for protocols such as SSDP, HTTP, HTTP/2, TLS, DTLS.

extensions.name: dns_response_name
extensions.description: Domain names extracting from a DNS response.

extensions.name: tls_metadata
extensions.description: The extension extracts additional information from TLS session - JA3/JA3S hashes, and so on.

extensions.name: tls_certificate
extensions.description: common_name and dns_name extracting from a TLS certificate.

extensions.name: ftp_cache
extensions.description: IP address caching and port obtained from the FTP-CONTROL stream for subsequent classification of the FTP-DATA stream (file transfer).

extensions.name: data_structure
extensions.description: Checks an unrecognized protocol for various patterns, such as the first two bytes to determine the payload size.

extensions.name: spid
extensions.description: The extension is responsible to classify tags based on statistical characteristics such as average packet size, bitrate, etc. It helps to classify tags which cannot be fully rely on _explicit_ classification.

extensions.name: classifier
extensions.description: The extension classifies Internet services and allows user to configure a set of metrics and classification techniques for each individually. Also, the extension sets flags for the session, such as is_final_protocol, is_final_service, is_offload, and so on.

extensions.name: sip_cache
extensions.description: The extension extracts additional information from SIP session and then classifies the child RTP/SRTP and RTCP/SRTCP flows assigning them tags of parent session.
